description: Chest pain or tightness can have many causes, but persistent or exertional chest discomfort should be evaluated by a cardiologist. A cardiologist specializes in diagnosing and treating diseases of the heart and blood vessels, including coronary artery disease, heart failure, and arrhythmias.
red_flags: pain radiating to jaw or left arm

description: Persistent high blood pressure (hypertension) requires monitoring and management by a cardiologist or internist. Uncontrolled hypertension increases risk of heart attack, stroke, and kidney damage.
red_flags: severe headache with very high BP

description: Persistent skin rashes, itching, or unusual skin changes should be evaluated by a dermatologist. Dermatologists specialize in conditions affecting the skin, hair, and nails, including eczema, psoriasis, acne, and skin cancer screening.

Severe or persistent acne and changing skin lesions warrant dermatological evaluation. Dermatologists can provide treatments ranging from topical medications to procedures, and can screen for skin cancer.
red_flags: mole that changes size, shape, or color rapidly

description: Symptoms like unexplained weight changes, fatigue, and temperature sensitivity may indicate thyroid disorders. An endocrinologist specializes in hormonal disorders including thyroid disease, diabetes, and metabolic conditions.
red_flags: thyroid storm symptoms: rapid heart rate, high fever, confusion

description: Diabetes requires ongoing management by an endocrinologist. They specialize in blood sugar regulation, insulin management, and preventing complications of diabetes including neuropathy and retinopathy.
red_flags: diabetic ketoacidosis symptoms: nausea, vomiting, fruity breath, confusion

description: Chronic digestive issues including acid reflux, abdominal pain, and bowel irregularities should be evaluated by a gastroenterologist. They specialize in diseases of the gastrointestinal tract, liver, and pancreas.

Chronic or severe headaches, especially migraines with aura, should be evaluated by a neurologist. Neurologists diagnose and treat disorders of the nervous system including migraines, epilepsy, and movement disorders.
red_flags: headache with stiff neck and fever

description: Persistent numbness, tingling, or weakness may indicate neurological conditions such as neuropathy, multiple sclerosis, or nerve compression. A neurologist can diagnose and manage these conditions.
red_flags: sudden numbness on one side of body

description: Joint pain, bone injuries, and musculoskeletal problems are treated by orthopedists (orthopedic surgeons). They specialize in the musculoskeletal system including bones, joints, ligaments, tendons, and muscles.
red_flags: inability to bear weight after injury

description: Chronic or severe back pain, especially with radiating symptoms like sciatica, should be evaluated by an orthopedist or spine specialist. They can diagnose disc problems, spinal stenosis, and other structural issues.
red_flags: back pain with loss of bladder/bowel control

description: Chronic breathing difficulties, persistent cough, or asthma management require a pulmonologist. They specialize in diseases of the lungs and respiratory system including COPD, asthma, and pulmonary fibrosis.
red_flags: coughing up large amounts of blood

description: Problems with the ears, nose, or throat — including hearing loss, chronic sinusitis, and swallowing difficulties — are treated by an ENT specialist (otolaryngologist).
red_flags: difficulty breathing due to throat swelling

description: Vision changes, eye pain, and visual disturbances should be evaluated by an ophthalmologist. They diagnose and treat eye diseases including glaucoma, cataracts, macular degeneration, and retinal disorders.
red_flags: sudden onset of many floaters with flashes

description: Persistent mental health symptoms like depression, anxiety, or panic attacks are treated by psychiatrists. They are medical doctors who can prescribe medication and provide comprehensive mental health treatment plans.
Urinary tract problems, kidney stones, and reproductive health issues in men are treated by urologists. They specialize in diseases of the urinary system and male reproductive system.

Autoimmune and inflammatory conditions like rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, and fibromyalgia are managed by rheumatologists. They specialize in diseases that affect joints, muscles, and the immune system.
red_flags: rash with joint pain and organ involvement

description: Unexplained symptoms like weight loss, persistent fatigue, lumps, or unusual bleeding may warrant evaluation by an oncologist. They specialize in the diagnosis and treatment of cancer.

Women's reproductive health issues including menstrual disorders, pregnancy care, and menopause management are handled by gynecologists. They also perform cancer screenings and manage hormonal conditions.
red_flags: heavy bleeding soaking through pads hourly

description: Kidney diseases, chronic kidney disease management, and electrolyte imbalances are treated by nephrologists. They specialize in kidney function, dialysis, and kidney transplant care.
red_flags: severe swelling with shortness of breath

description: Chronic allergies, recurrent allergic reactions, and immune deficiencies are managed by allergists/immunologists. They can perform allergy testing and develop management plans including immunotherapy.
red_flags: anaphylaxis: throat swelling, difficulty breathing, rapid pulse

description: For routine health checkups, preventive care, vaccinations, and non-specific symptoms, a general practitioner (GP) or family medicine doctor is the appropriate first point of contact. They can also refer to specialists if needed.

Health concerns for children and adolescents — including developmental issues, childhood illnesses, and vaccinations — are managed by pediatricians. They specialize in medical care from infancy through adolescence.
red_flags: child with high fever and rash
